description: Vectorized NumPy array access for ball attributes and bulk ball creation.

functions.name: create
functions.signature: itasca.ballarray.create (radii: array float{ball}, centroids: array float{ball,dim}, **kwds) -> array int{ball} IDs of the newly created balls.
functions.description: Create balls from two arrays. The first array must contain the radii, and the second array the positions. The arrays must be the correct shape. Additional keyword arguments can be specified which will set the newly created ball attributes. The keyword arguments values can be scalars, vecs or arrays of 1 or two dimensions. The keyword argument props can be used to set ball properties, the value should be a dict of type {str: value}. The keyword argument extra can be used to set extra variables, the value should be a dict of type {int: value}. The props or extra dictionary values can be a 1 or 2 dimensional array of doubles, a number, a vec or a string.

parameters.name: **kwds
parameters.description: Optional keyword arguments accepted by the official API.

returns.type: array int{ball} IDs of the newly created balls
returns.description: array int{ball} IDs of the newly created balls

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
radii = np.full(10, 0.05)
centroids = np.zeros((10, it.dim()))
ids = it.ballarray.create(radii, centroids)
examples.description: Create balls in bulk from radius and centroid arrays.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.ballarray.extra (slot : int) -> array float{ball} or float{ball,3}.
functions.description: Get the ball extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballarray.extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: fill_damp
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the ball local damping. The array must be the correct shape.

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
data = np.empty_like(it.ballarray.damp())
it.ballarray.fill_damp(data)
examples.description: Fill a preallocated NumPy array to avoid allocating a new result array.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
The destination array must already have the correct shape and dtype.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.ballarray.set_extra (slot: int, data: array float{ball} or float{ball, 3}) -> None.
functions.description: Set the ball extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:

values = it.ballarray.set_extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.ballarray.set_group (membership: array bool{ball}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None.
functions.description: Set ball group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding ball to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballarray.set_group()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: set_pos
functions.description: Set the ball centroid location from an array.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
positions = it.ballarray.pos()
positions[:, 2] += 0.1
it.ballarray.set_pos(positions)
examples.description: Modify positions in bulk and write them back.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
